title: Immediate Family and Police Station Representation
metaTitle: Can Immediate Family Instruct a Solicitor?
metaDescription: Who may instruct a police station solicitor on someone else's behalf — immediate family, current custody only, and detainee consent.
contentHtml:
Who can instruct?

Immediate family (parent, spouse or partner, child, or sibling) may contact a solicitor when someone is in current police custody in Kent. Friends cannot instruct on someone else's behalf.

Consent

The detainee must confirm they want the solicitor when contacted — PACE Code C. We do not provide status updates or help with arrests from yesterday or missing-person enquiries.

Read more

author: Robert Cashman
